exemplar.title: How does Holden Caulfieldâs psychological portrait in J.D. Salingerâs novel âThe Catcher in the Ryeâ characterize Post-War American society in the 1950s?

detailedExemplarData.title: How does Holden Caulfieldâs psychological portrait in J.D. Salingerâs novel âThe Catcher in the Ryeâ characterize Post-War American society in the 1950s?

subject.title: English B
subject.description:
**IB English B** is a **Group 2** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **language acquisition through five prescribed themes: identities, experiences, human ingenuity, social organization, and sharing the planet**, with a strong focus on **developing receptive, productive, and interactive communication skills in English as a second language**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2025**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| **Assessment** | **Format** | **SL** | **HL** | **Weighting** |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **Paper 1** | Productive skills - writing | 1 hour | 1 hour 15 minutes | 25% |
| **Paper 2** | Receptive skills - listening and reading comprehension | 1 hour 45 minutes | 2 hours | 50% |
| **Individual Oral** | Internal assessment - oral communication | 15 min prep + 10 min exam | 15 min prep + 10 min exam | 25% |

strengths: The topic is communicated accurately and effectively, with consistently clear and appropriate focus throughout the essay.
The research question is clearly stated, narrowly focused, and remains the guiding thread of each analytical section.
Section headings and topic sentences reinforce the link between Holdenâs psychology and post-war American society, demonstrating an effective organizational method.
weaknesses: Methodology lacks a fully explicit rationale for source selection and contains inconsistent citation style.
Heavy reliance on non-peer-reviewed websites and occasional digressions into informal definitions dilute scholarly rigour.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Although the methodology strand shows minor inconsistencies and the citation style is not perfectly consistent, the student communicates the topic and purpose with precision, frames a narrowly focused research question, and structures the investigation in a coherent, effective way. These strengths meet the descriptors for Band 5â6, and the very minor methodological issues do not outweigh the overall excellence in focus and method, warranting the top mark of 6.

strengths: Source material from the primary text and relevant secondary articles is integrated clearly into the argument, with each quotation followed by an explanatory link to the research focus.
Subject knowledge is coherent and well structured, showing a firm grasp of the novelâs plot, historical context, and thematic intersections.
Terminology and concepts (âalienation,â âphoniness,â âconsumerismâ) are defined and applied consistently and appropriately throughout.
weaknesses: Over-reliance on dictionary entries for term definitions at points, rather than deeper scholarly sources.
Minor misinterpretations when generalizing anecdotes to broader social claims.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The studentâs application of source material is clearly relevant and appropriate, subject knowledge is thorough and coherent, and terminology is used accurately and consistently. While there is some reliance on dictionary definitions, the overall use of scholarly and primary evidence supports a Band 5â6 placement. Given the high level of clarity and effective integration of sources, the criterion warrants the top score of 6.

strengths: Research evidence is broad, consistently relevant to the research question, and marshalled effectively in each analytical strand.
Analysis employs a clear claimâevidenceâcomment structure, with textual quotations linked persuasively to socio-historical interpretation.
The discussion is coherent and focused, and the conclusion synthesises the three strands into a unified answer to the research question.
weaknesses: Some speculative leaps (e.g., interpreting the duck motif without reference to established literary criticism) show minor gaps in scholarly validation.
Counter-argument and deeper evaluation of source reliability are limited, reducing the nuance of the overall discussion.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The essay demonstrates sustained relevance of research, strong analytical depth, and a focused, coherent argument in discussion and evaluation, fitting Band 10â12 descriptors. Minor speculative elements and limited source evaluation prevent a flawless 12, but an 11 properly reflects the high quality of critical thinking displayed.

strengths: The report follows an appropriate extended-essay structure with a title page, table of contents, clearly labelled sections, conclusion, and bibliography.
Headings and sub-headings guide the reader logically through the argument.
All formal elements (title, structure components) are present and correctly placed.
weaknesses: Page numbers are missing, and URL breaks in the bibliography impede readability.
A small number of typographical errors (e.g., misspelt character names) and abrupt transitions disrupt the formal presentation.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student meets all major structural requirements for top-band presentation. Although there are several formal layout and proofreading issues, the integrity of the overall format remains intact. These minor lapses do not outweigh the completeness and appropriateness of the structure, supporting the maximum score of 4.

strandSelections.reasoning:
The research question is explicitly stated on the title page and restated in the introduction.  It is narrowly framed (linking a single protagonist to a defined historical context) and remains the guiding thread of each analytical section.  
Evidence of focus appears in the structure: three body sections each tackle one aspect of the question and end with mini-conclusions tying the finding back to post-war society.  
Focus slips at times when lengthy dictionary or pop-culture explanations are inserted, but these do not seriously undermine the clarity of the question or its connection to discussion, meriting a strong score within the 5â6 band.

Analytical paragraphs follow a clear claim-evidence-comment pattern, linking textual quotations to socio-historical interpretation.  Reasoning is coherent and usually persuasive, though it occasionally drifts into speculation (e.g., interpreting ducks solely as fear of adulthood).  
Minor gaps prevent the analysis from reaching âexcellentâ, but overall depth is solidly âgoodâ, meriting the mid-high score in the 4â6 band.

Quotations from the novel are well chosen to illustrate Holdenâs perceptions of Mr Haas, D.B., Stradlater, etc., and each is followed by explanation that relates to the research focus.  Secondary sources (e.g., History.com, academic article on social status) are generally relevant, though some dictionary links add little analytical value.  
Because sources are woven into argument but not critically compared or evaluated, the application is mostly, not consistently, appropriate.  A 4 within the 3â4 band is therefore appropriate.

The layout is generally reader-friendly, using headings, a table layout for contents and consistent font choices.  However, evidence of formal pagination is absent in the excerpt, and citation formatting is inconsistent (URL breaks, missing hanging indents).  
Because the basic elements are present but not fully correct, the work aligns with the middle band for layout, meriting 2 points.

The student demonstrates a sound grasp of the novelâs plot, historical background and broader cultural concepts such as consumerism and religious conformity.  Arguments show coherent, although sometimes simplified, understanding of how these factors intersect.  
Misinterpretations are minor (e.g., conflating isolated anecdotes with sweeping societal claims), and the knowledge remains clear overall.  Effective but not sophisticated use of sources indicates a solid, mid-level competence.

The essay follows a conventional EE structure: title page, abstract elements within the introduction, clearly labelled body sections, conclusion and bibliography.  A table of contents with page indicators is provided.  Headings and sub-headings guide the reader logically.  
All required components are present and placed appropriately, so a top-band score within PresentationâStructure is warranted.

The investigation marshals a broad range of primary evidence and contextual references that are clearly linked to the research question.  While some sources lack academic rigour, their collective relevance to the argument is clear.  
The breadth and pertinence of evidence exceed âadequateâ but fall short of the sustained scholarly rigour required for the top band, justifying a strong 5 within the 4â6 range.

The student introduces the investigation as an exploration of how Holden Caulfieldâs psychology mirrors post-war American society and maintains that focus consistently from introduction through conclusion.  
â¢  The opening paragraph clearly identifies the literary text, historical period and central concepts (âphoninessâ, alienation, trust issues).  
â¢  Subsequent section headings and topic sentences repeatedly connect Holdenâs traits to 1950s America, demonstrating a purposeful alignment between topic and purpose.  
Although occasional digressions into dictionary definitions or contemporary psychology blogs dilute the academic tone, the overall communication of topic and purpose is clear and appropriate, warranting a high-band placement.

Each main section ends with a concise restatement of how the evidence answers the research question, and the final conclusion synthesises the three strands of argument.  Counter-argument and evaluation of source reliability are limited, so the discussion is effective rather than fully nuanced.  
Consequently, the essay meets descriptors for the âgoodâ band, earning a 5.

The essay cites the primary text and a sizeable bibliography of secondary web sources, indicating some effort at informed selection; however, many references are journalistic sites, blogs or dictionary pages that carry limited scholarly weight.  
There is no explicit explanation of why particular sources or approaches were chosen, nor any comment on research limitations.  In-text citation style is inconsistent and page references to the novel are given without edition details.  
Hence methodology is âmostly completeâ rather than fully effective, aligning with the 3â4 band and justifying a mid-band mark.

Literary and sociological terms such as âalienationâ, âmonologueâ, âconsumerismâ and âphoninessâ are defined and generally applied correctly.  Occasional over-reliance on dictionary definitions and informal phrasing (âphony peopleâ) lowers academic consistency, yet misuse is rare.  
Terminology is therefore mostly accurate and appropriate, fitting securely within the 3â4 band.

annotations.comment: The inclusion of a clear title and explicit word count demonstrates attention to formatting requirements and helps set the context immediately.
Consider adding an abstract summarizing the scope, methodology, and key findings to conform with EE requirements and improve reader orientation.
The table of contents is comprehensive and correctly formatted, ensuring a logical navigation structure for the reader.
Page numbers are not visibly printed in the footer, which hinders easy reference; ensure consistent pagination throughout.
The introduction effectively situates the novel within its historical context and applies relevant secondary sources to frame the research focus.
There is heavy reliance on dictionary definitions for key terms; consider integrating more scholarly interpretations to deepen analysis.
To strengthen methodological rigor, explicitly explain your rationale for selecting specific primary and secondary sources.
The research question is restated with precision and your outline of three analytical strands provides a clear roadmap for the essay.
Page references to the novel are given without edition details and citation style is inconsistent; this undermines precision.
Include full edition details for the novel and adopt a consistent citation format for inâtext references.
The analysis of Holdenâs monologue is well structured around claimâevidenceâcomment, effectively linking textual detail to thematic interpretation.
Several contemporary psychology studies are cited without justification of their relevance to a 1950s text; this weakens historical validity.
Critically assess whether modern psychological research can be validly applied to the novelâs original historical context.
Cultural context on celebrity culture is detailed and enriches the discussion of D.B.âs Hollywood career shift.
Several quotations lack page numbers and contexts, reducing traceability; ensure each citation is complete.
A proofread is needed: the character name âHorwitzâ appears misspelled as âHrowitz,â which can undermine credibility.
Proofread all character names and key terms to ensure accuracy and maintain reader confidence.
The symbolic interpretation of the ducks question insightfully connects Holdenâs fear of change to broader societal anxieties.
The duck motif analysis is speculative without reference to literary scholarship; strengthen by citing critical sources.
Consider integrating established literary criticism on the duck motif to validate your interpretation.
The episode with Allieâs glove is used effectively to illustrate Holdenâs sense of loss and alienation.
Transitions between examples sometimes lack topic sentences, which can disrupt flow; consider clearer signposting.
The contrast between religious conformity and Holdenâs atheist stance provides a compelling layer of thematic depth.
When citing church membership growth, add a precise source and statistical data to substantiate the claim.
The theoretical framing of trust issues is well supported by definitions and connects effectively back to societal critique.
The shift to the next section on trust issues feels abrupt; add a bridging sentence to improve narrative coherence.
The conclusion ties together Holdenâs internal and external traits to articulate a coherent overall argument.
The final paragraph reiterates earlier points without adding new insight; consider refining to highlight broader implications.
Bibliography entries show varied styles and missing hanging indents; standardize formatting to a single citation style.
Adopt and apply consistently one recognized reference style (e.g., APA or MLA) throughout the bibliography.
The bibliography relies heavily on non-peer-reviewed websites and blogs, which limits scholarly depth; seek more academic sources.
The extensive list of sources reflects thorough research, demonstrating a strong effort to gather varied perspectives.
Line breaks within URLs impede readability and usability; ensure each link is continuous.
Verify all URLs for functionality and present them in a consistent, unbroken format.
